text: PagedAttention eliminates KV cache memory waste through virtual-memory-style paging.
PagedAttention addresses the memory management bottleneck identified in the inference problem analysis.
vLLM was built around PagedAttention as its core innovation.
SGLang's RadixAttention extends the paged approach with automatic prefix discovery via radix tree.
FlashAttention reduces HBM access for attention computation through IO-aware tiling.
vLLM uses FlashAttention/FlashInfer as its attention kernel backend.
SGLang uses FlashInfer (based on FlashAttention principles) for paged attention kernels.
Continuous batching solves GPU underutilization during the memory-bound decode phase.
vLLM implements continuous batching with iteration-level scheduling.
SGLang implements continuous batching with cache-aware scheduling.
Speculative decoding addresses the sequential decode bottleneck by parallelizing verification.
Disaggregated serving resolves the prefill-decode interference by physical separation.
vLLM supports disaggregated prefill/decode deployment.
MoE serving introduces unique memory/communication challenges beyond standard dense model inference.
TensorRT-LLM integrates FlashAttention kernels and fuses them with surrounding ops.
Ollama/llama.cpp serves the local inference niche with different trade-offs than production systems.
Benchmark data compares vLLM throughput and latency against alternatives.
Benchmark data compares SGLang throughput and latency against alternatives.
Benchmark data compares TensorRT-LLM peak performance against alternatives.
KV cache is the central memory constraint introduced by the autoregressive inference problem.
